title: Evangelion 30 Q Review
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] Evangelion 3.0 (Q) Review Post by 2Girls1Frankie » Wed Jan 16, 2013 1:47 am Please excuse my lack of posts, I keep this account on hand in the case that I want to discuss elements of the series or anything at all Eva-related, however I just haven't found the time. Today I want to give you an in-depth review of what I just saw today, the subbed camrip of Evangelion 3.0. There are spoilers here, big ones. If you haven't seen the movie yet I would highly recommend you view that first. A subbed rip isn't hard to come by. I have seen everything in the Evangelion franchise. From Death and Rebirth, to cut-scenes found exclusively in Japanese Evangelion-themed slot machines, just for novelty's sake, so I am completely assured that I haven't missed a thing in the series and I am confident in saying that this movie is not only very frustrating to watch, it's also immensely disappointing. Let's cover the positives, Anno maintains his excellent attention to detail, my favorite examples of these details were in the destroyed Nerv headquarters, where familiar areas are burned out and walls are cracked and riddled with bulletholes. The red sandy and barren earth is also riddled with destroyed buildings and structures, it's all very bleak looking. The animation during the action sequences flow well and it's a real spectacle watching the Evas flip around and crash with explosions everywhere, all done so fluently. You won't find speed lines here. I especially enjoy the first 7 minutes, which, according to Gainax, is even scientifically accurate, down to the rotation of the Eva in space and the movement of the jettisoned equipment. There are three sequences that stand out, the first one in Earth's orbit, the Wunder battle and the Climax. All of which are spectacles. As for the dialogue, Shinji whines a lot in the movie but I actually feel his pain here. He spends most of the movie saying "What's going on?" and "I'm so confused", almost like he's a member of the audience, it was kind of surreal, almost as if Shinji himself was a spectator to all of this and really he is, he doesn't do anything until the end of the movie, we'll get to that because it contains the worst part of the film. Although the movie itself is an hour and thirty minutes long, you rarely feel its length. Except in certain talky scenes, most of which I felt didn't advance the plot in the right direction. I fully expected to receive some confirmation on my theories about what was going down, in the scenes with Kaworu I was yelling at my screen for Anno to ditch the psuedo-philosophical jargon and give me a concrete explanation. If ever a movie needed exposition, it's Evangelion 3.0. An example of this was after the piano duet, which is a good scene, when Kaworu and Shinji are looking at the stars. What do they talk about? How much Shinji likes the stars. Shinji could be asking direct questions here and possibly receive some answers, but he never asks. The same with a scene where he plays Shogi with Fuyutski, he fails to ask him imperative questions about what happened in the 14 years that passed, how Nerv was destroyed, or about this war between WILLE and Nerv, instead Fuyutski explains to Shinji about his mother. Granted, this is important, however why doesn't Shinji ask more questions? He asks all of his questions to the WILLE personnel and not to those who actually know what's going on. Our old friends have virtually no character. Misato is a cold middle-aged woman now with very little personality beyond barking out orders and showing clear contempt for Shinji. She has essentially turned into the Gendo of WILLE and only has one or two scenes where she shows some humanity. Ritsuko is a bit nicer, but still on the same level. We see elder versions of Maya and Nerdy Glasses Guy whose name escapes me at the moment, but they're just colder versions of their past selves as well. Asuka spends the entirety of her dialogue calling Shinji stupid, I'm not joking. Every line from Asuka is her calling Shinji a brat, stupid or barking out orders or mission objectives. Rei is virtually invisible in the film. The two main characters are Kaworu and Shinji. The newer characters show contempt for Shinji as well and spend the entire movie talking in technobabble and following orders. The only character with some personality is Toji's sister (and Kaworu), who is a head medical officer and while she is definitely a kinder person to Shinji than anyone else, she has her lines earlier in the movie and never speaks again. I assure you that any answers you may have been pining for about what occurred post-3rd Impact are seldom, if ever answered. Here is a list of imperative plot-points that I felt needed to be explained in the movie: - Lilith's "corpse" and the skulls in Central Dogma. - What the "Nemesis Series" is. (If they were Angels, wouldn't they call them Angels?) - 95% of what occurred in the 14 years after Eva-01's awakening. - The Giant Rei Head. - The details of how Nerv was destroyed and who or what destroyed it and the details of the war between WILLE and Nerv. - The Eva Mark .06 (Kaworu claims that it was made into an autonomous unit and brought to ruin. However it's revealed to be an Angel after, so did he lie, or was he unaware? How was it possible that the entirety of this Angel was a core?) - The other "Earth" Shinji sees when Kaworu's showing "the truth" to him. - The Eva-Like body parts scattered across the ruins of Tokyo-3. - The oblique pyramid altar in the sky and it's significance. (If the Impacts happen regardless of location, this altar and it's significance needs to be explained.) - What the Adams/Adams' Vessels are. Or why or if there is more than one Adam. - Where Kaji is and what befell him. (Aoba mentions his name once, in the past tense, he may be dead.) - The population of the Earth. (Speculating here, if WILLE has a fleet of battleships and a full staff, there must be people however we see no citizens and other countries aren't mentioned, nor are governments or other cities, it's to be assumed that the world was very nearly destroyed and WILLE personnel may be the last on Earth.) - Mark .09 and it's connection to WILLE and the AAA Wunder. (They refer to it as Wunder's "Original Master") - What Kaworu actually is and his true motives. - Why Gendo killed the old men of SEELE and why they were so willing to comply just before the Instrumentality project was initiated. - How the pilots maintained their exact appearance and body types after 14 years. (Asuka gives us only one explanation for this: The Curse of Eva. That is all of the explanation we get for this phenomenon, Shinji's hair and fingernails don't even grow after all of that time in the Eva.) These are just a few things that are either glossed over in one-liners, or are completely left out of the story all together. The frustrating thing about leaving these pieces out is that if they were explained more elaborately, we would be able to paint a much clearer picture of the plot. Regarding Mari. We still have no bearing on who this girl is exactly, what her motives are or how she knows what these Adams' Vessels are. She seemingly knows what Gendo's plan is (which she makes perfectly clear she knows during the climax), or how she's able to eat lunch in her Eva. The last one was a nitpick, however it's about as rational as drinking a can of Pepsi through a straw underwater. Now I'm going to tell you all the single dumbest moment in the entire franchise. Yes, dumber than in Magmadiver when the entire plot revolved around Unit 02 needing a cumbersome suit to enter a volcano and Unit 01 surviving jumping in after it to save Asuka from an Angel at the last minute. That made zero sense, this makes even less, plus the stakes are far, far higher this time. Beyond Shinji being emotionally (or mentally) unstable, Shinji and Kaworu pilot Eva-13 to pull two Spears out of Lilith to reverse 3rd Impact. Kaworu is the one who convinces him to do this and he is also a sort of mentor to Shinji. Shinji cannot pilot Eva due to a choker installed on his neck, if he pilots he dies. Kaworu takes the choker off of Shinji before the last mission and puts it on himself. I actually don't have a problem with this, Kaworu could have used a little foresight and thought that since he can't die, that he should wear it as a last resort. Halfway to pull the Spears, Asuka attacks along with Mari, they fight Eva-13 and halfway, Kaworu notices that the Spears are the same(?). He tells Shinji to stop repeatedly, telling him that they aren't the Spears they came for. Shinji retorts with stupid lines like "Let's just pull the Spears quickly!" and "That's why I got in the Eva!" Earlier Shinji told Kaworu that he seemed to know everything, Kaworu gave Shinji the information about the mission in the first place. Yet, when he and everyone around him continually tells him to not pull the spears he knew nothing about 20 minutes earlier, he continues the mission even going as far as to cut the controls from Kaworu and do it himself. Naturally, this causes Fourth Impact. As a stand alone movie it's impossible to grasp the full picture, you may even hate it. Of course anyone going into the movie must have seen the previous movies and in the context of the movies, this one was just okay. I like that in every movie the series steers itself farther from the subject material and does its own thing, it is supposed to be a retelling after all. This movie was the first step in truly revamping the ending to it all and unfortunately it doesn't really work. It brings up far more questions than answers and honestly, I'm worried about a conclusion. I feel as if Gainax dug a hole for itself with this movie. If you're a fan of Evangelion, you'll like the movie like I did. I was disappointed, sure, however I am looking forward to how they handle Evangelion's conclusion. They can right a lot of wrongs with 4.0 You can (not) repeat. Thank you for reading this review! Congratulations!

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] Evangelion 3.0 (Q) Review; (READ BEFORE WATCH Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 16, 2013 1:57 am 2Girls1Frankie wrote: Please excuse my lack of posts, I keep this account on hand in the case that I want to discuss elements of the series or anything at all Eva-related, however I just haven't found the time.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] Evangelion 3.0 (Q) Review Post by Electric Sachiel » Wed Jan 16, 2013 4:56 am 2Girls1Frankie wrote: I feel as if Gainax dug a hole for itself with this movie. NOT APPEARING IN THIS MOVIE : - The "Key of Nebuchadnezzar"

[]: Post by Reichu » Wed Jan 16, 2013 4:58 am Gendo has the case with him when he's talking to the Lilith head, too.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] Evangelion 3.0 (Q) Review Post by 2Girls1Frankie » Wed Jan 16, 2013 8:28 am Electric Sachiel wrote: For the last time Evangelion fans, studio Gainax has nothing to do with these new movies. I suppose you really haven't been paying attention around here or been watching these movies closely? Studio Khara is the principle studio behind the production of these recent Rebuild movies. As for the Key of Nebuchadnezzar, it does appear in the movie in the form of the case that Kaji delivered to Gendo's office. In 3.0 we see that very same brief case apparently still sitting on Gendo's desk.

[]: Post by Shinoyami65 » Wed Jan 16, 2013 8:37 am That's not Lilith's body on the Moon, it is apparently the Mark.06, under construction and presumably wrapped in a white tarpaulin (unless its real skin is white tarpaulin like Lilith's is marshmallow) and wearing a mask similar to Lilith's. This is before they put all the armor and stuff on. In 2.0 they place Mark.06's armor on her off-screen and she leaves her lunar trench to head towards her ultimate doom.

[]: Re: [SPOILERS] Evangelion 3.0 (Q) Review Post by 2Girls1Frankie » Wed Jan 16, 2013 8:39 am Removing the Not Appearing In this Movie section, so people can focus on the body of the review and not nit-picky things that have absolutely nothing to do with the overall critique. (All of which were more things that were glossed over/unexplained.) Not trying to be a d-bag, but I really hope people here realize that there's a thin line between ambiguity and bad storytelling.

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Jan 16, 2013 11:12 am If you want to participate here, marching in with a chip on your shoulder and starting your own special snowflake review thread when we already have dedicated threads for posting personal review/reactions, refusing to read any of the threads addressing many of your issues, only responding to people pointing out mistakes in your post with an attitude, and overlooking the insightful post Reichu made are not the ways to go about it. If as you say you don't post here and don't care about this place, why should we care about bending to your whims? You bring up several good points and several not so good ones, and all have been and are being discussed in various threads. If you're too good to actually read and participate, then maybe you're just too good for us. So kindly descend to our lowly level or keep your rarified atmosphere to yourself, please. Also, welcome to Evageeks.

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Jan 16, 2013 2:06 pm I've thought it over a bit and I was too brazen in my post too. Honestly your post is fine, it could just probably get merged into one of the Reviews / Reactions thread. But that's all of course up to mod discretion. I just mostly got peeved at some of the wording in the followup responses, but anyway it didn't require me to go quite that far. Sorry for overreacting, I tend to be a bit of a jerk myself.

[]: Post by Darkwing » Wed Jan 16, 2013 4:34 pm On the one hand we have another movie to see some explinations. On the other, we may still not get them. On one hand trying to figure stuff out is one of the favorate passtimes of this fandom. On the other, we may never know if we're right without confirmation. Anyways welcome, and neat review.

[]: Post by Electric Sachiel » Wed Jan 16, 2013 6:55 pm I give Gainax does have some small involvement with the production of these films. But its clear that Studio Khara is leaidng the way with the bulk of the production work. Despite what I said earlier about the nitpicks, your critique of the film is sound. You pretty much backed up all your personal points that you spoke of. Nice work there. I share the similar views as you do with 3.0's intentional or unintentional ambiguity. Theres too little plot and character exposition in the film to make this one easy to understand and easy to fall in love with. Even for us Eva fans this film is all over the place. Its true that they delivered on their promise with the introduction of new material to the franchise, but the bulk of it was introduced with little to no clear explanation. Its clear that Anno ain't trying to hold our hands while telling the story anymore. Like Ridley Scott he loves to introduce imagery and plot points that are totally ambiguous. If anything I liken 3.0 to be an action packed and entertaining viral advertisement for the franchise rather than movie that sits down and takes its time to answer questions for the fans and audience. Sort of like how we were letdown by this past year's movie Prometheus I guess..... In that sense I guess 2.0 is definitely a more balanced film with character and plot development that compliments each other nicely. 3.0 not so much..... I still enjoyed the film for what it was though. Can't wait for its eventual theatrical release stateside or on blu ray and dvd.

[]: Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 17, 2013 2:57 pm OP says they won't be checking the thread anymore, so locking at their request.

[]: Post by Seele00TextOnly » Wed Jan 16, 2013 2:06 pm I've thought it over a bit and I was too brazen in my post too. Honestly your post is fine, it could just probably get merged into one of the Reviews / Reactions thread. But that's all of course up to mod discretion. I just mostly got peeved at some of the wording in the followup responses, but anyway it didn't require me to go quite that far. Sorry for overreacting, I tend to be a bit of a jerk myself.

[]: Post by Darkwing » Wed Jan 16, 2013 4:34 pm On the one hand we have another movie to see some explinations. On the other, we may still not get them. On one hand trying to figure stuff out is one of the favorate passtimes of this fandom. On the other, we may never know if we're right without confirmation. Anyways welcome, and neat review.

[]: Post by Electric Sachiel » Wed Jan 16, 2013 6:55 pm I give Gainax does have some small involvement with the production of these films. But its clear that Studio Khara is leaidng the way with the bulk of the production work. Despite what I said earlier about the nitpicks, your critique of the film is sound. You pretty much backed up all your personal points that you spoke of. Nice work there. I share the similar views as you do with 3.0's intentional or unintentional ambiguity. Theres too little plot and character exposition in the film to make this one easy to understand and easy to fall in love with. Even for us Eva fans this film is all over the place. Its true that they delivered on their promise with the introduction of new material to the franchise, but the bulk of it was introduced with little to no clear explanation. Its clear that Anno ain't trying to hold our hands while telling the story anymore. Like Ridley Scott he loves to introduce imagery and plot points

Article: Evangelion 30 Q Review
 of new material to the franchise, but the bulk of it was introduced with little to no clear explanation. Its clear that Anno ain't trying to hold our hands while telling the story anymore. Like Ridley Scott he loves to introduce imagery and plot points that are totally ambiguous. If anything I liken 3.0 to be an action packed and entertaining viral advertisement for the franchise rather than movie that sits down and takes its time to answer questions for the fans and audience. Sort of like how we were letdown by this past year's movie Prometheus I guess..... In that sense I guess 2.0 is definitely a more balanced film with character and plot development that compliments each other nicely. 3.0 not so much..... I still enjoyed the film for what it was though. Can't wait for its eventual theatrical release stateside or on blu ray and dvd.
